tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

response.delta: Let me start by navigating to the Effect blog to scrape the articles, and
also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and
checking the available schemas for Person objects.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: delete-object
tools.description: Deletes the object.

obj.description:

tools.name: update-object

obj.description:

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by navigating to the Effect blog to scrape the articles, and also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas for Person objects.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

            - paragraph [ref=e228]: Hi Effecters!
            - paragraph [ref=e229]:
              - text: Welcome back to This Week In Effect (
              - strong [ref=e230]: TWIE
              - text: ) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.
            - paragraph [ref=e231]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.
            - paragraph [ref=e232]: To get started, below you’ll find links to our documentation as well as our guide for installing Effect. Enjoy!

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

            - paragraph [ref=e228]: Hi Effecters!
            - paragraph [ref=e229]:
              - text: Welcome back to This Week In Effect (
              - strong [ref=e230]: TWIE
              - text: ) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.
            - paragraph [ref=e231]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.
            - paragraph [ref=e232]: To get started, below you’ll find links to our documentation as well as our guide for installing Effect. Enjoy!

                  - strong [ref=e268]:
                    - text: "Breaking:"
                    - code [ref=e269]: ServiceMap
                    - text: renamed to
                    - code [ref=e270]: Context
                  - text: ": The"
                  - code [ref=e271]: ServiceMap
                  - text: module has been renamed back to
                  - code [ref=e272]: Context
                  - text: ", realigning with Effect v3 naming conventions. This is one of the most impactful changes of the week for anyone already building on the v4 beta."
              - listitem [ref=e273]:
                - paragraph [ref=e274]:
                  - strong [ref=e275]: IndexedDB expansion
                  - text: ": Added streaming and offset support to IDB queries, compound index improvements, encoded types for IDB queries, a"
                  - code [ref=e276]: .reverse()
                  - text: method to IDB selects, a
                  - code [ref=e277]: rebuild
                  - text: API for IDB databases, an improved transaction API, and support for customizing IDB durability.
                  - code [ref=e278]: Model.Class
                  - text: can now also be used directly for IndexedDB schemas.
              - listitem [ref=e279]:
                - paragraph [ref=e280]:
                  - strong [ref=e281]: Schema additions & fixes
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e282]: StringFromBase64
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e283]: StringFromBase64Url
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e284]: StringFromHex
                  - text: ", and"
                  - code [ref=e285]: StringFromUriComponent
                  - text: schemas for decoding encoded strings into UTF-8, improved
                  - code [ref=e286]: Schema.encodeKeys
                  - text: typing, fixed several missing
                  - code [ref=e287]: ~rebuild.out
                  - text: types, fixed duplicate identifier reference reuse, added a compile-time
                  - code [ref=e288]: MissingSelfGeneric
                  - text: error for Class APIs, and fixed
                  - code [ref=e289]: "@category"
                  - text: JSDoc tags.
              - listitem [ref=e290]:
                - paragraph [ref=e291]:
                  - strong [ref=e292]: RPC & Entity
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e293]: rpc
                  - code [ref=e294]: ConnectionHooks
                  - text: ", fixed"
                  - code [ref=e295]: Entity.keepAlive
                  - text: ", and improved defect handling when building Entity handlers."
              - listitem [ref=e296]:
                - paragraph [ref=e297]:
                  - strong [ref=e298]: Runtime & type inference
                  - text: ": Used"
                  - code [ref=e299]: NoInfer
                  - text: in
                  - code [ref=e300]: Layer
                  - text: constructors to prevent type erasure, fixed cache constructor inference by moving the
                  - code [ref=e301]: lookup
                  - text: option, fixed type inference for
                  - code [ref=e302]: Effect.retry
                  - text: when the
                  - code [ref=e303]: times
                  - text: option is provided, and fixed
                  - code [ref=e304]: Stream.toQueue
                  - text: types.
              - listitem [ref=e305]:
                - paragraph [ref=e306]:
                  - strong [ref=e307]: CLI & HTTP
                  - text: ": Fixed unstable CLI boolean flags, underlined active labels in CLI multi-select prompts, added support for"
                  - code [ref=e308]: form-urlencoded
                  - text: in the OpenAPI generator’s HTTP client format, and added a docs section on customizing schema error responses in
                  - code [ref=e309]: HttpApi
                  - text: .
              - listitem [ref=e310]:
                - paragraph [ref=e311]:
                  - strong [ref=e312]: PostgreSQL & internals
                  - text: ": Guarded transaction acquire failures in"
                  - code [ref=e313]: PgClient.fromPool
                  - text: ", returned resolvers directly from"
                  - code [ref=e314]: SqlModel.makeResolvers
                  - text: ", added"
                  - code [ref=e315]: KeyValueStore.layerSql
                  - text: ", and made"
                  - code [ref=e316]: Unify.unify
                  - text: work with the
                  - code [ref=e317]: Layer
                  - text: module.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: browser_navigate_forward
tools.description: Go forward to the next page

tools.name: browser_pdf_save
tools.description: Save page as PDF

filename.description: File name to save the pdf to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.pdf` if not specified.

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

raw.description: Whether to return without compression (in PNG format). Default is false, which returns a JPEG image.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to screenshot the element. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If element is provided, ref must be provided too.

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If ref is provided, element must be provided too.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startRef.description: Exact source element reference from the page snapshot

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endRef.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: browser_hover

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into editable element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

tools.name: browser_select_option

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by navigating to the Effect blog to scrape the articles, and also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas for Person objects.

            - paragraph [ref=e228]: Hi Effecters!
            - paragraph [ref=e229]:
              - text: Welcome back to This Week In Effect (
              - strong [ref=e230]: TWIE
              - text: ) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.
            - paragraph [ref=e231]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.
            - paragraph [ref=e232]: To get started, below you’ll find links to our documentation as well as our guide for installing Effect. Enjoy!
            - list [ref=e233]:
              - listitem [ref=e234]:

              - text: Here are the main changes that landed in
              - code [ref=e264]: effect-smol

            - list [ref=e265]:
              - listitem [ref=e266]:
                - paragraph [ref=e267]:
                  - strong [ref=e268]:
                    - text: "Breaking:"
                    - code [ref=e269]: ServiceMap
                    - text: renamed to
                    - code [ref=e270]: Context
                  - text: ": The"
                  - code [ref=e271]: ServiceMap
                  - text: module has been renamed back to
                  - code [ref=e272]: Context
                  - text: ", realigning with Effect v3 naming conventions. This is one of the most impactful changes of the week for anyone already building on the v4 beta."
              - listitem [ref=e273]:
                - paragraph [ref=e274]:
                  - strong [ref=e275]: IndexedDB expansion
                  - text: ": Added streaming and offset support to IDB queries, compound index improvements, encoded types for IDB queries, a"
                  - code [ref=e276]: .reverse()
                  - text: method to IDB selects, a
                  - code [ref=e277]: rebuild
                  - text: API for IDB databases, an improved transaction API, and support for customizing IDB durability.
                  - code [ref=e278]: Model.Class
                  - text: can now also be used directly for IndexedDB schemas.
              - listitem [ref=e279]:
                - paragraph [ref=e280]:
                  - strong [ref=e281]: Schema additions & fixes
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e282]: StringFromBase64
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e283]: StringFromBase64Url
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e284]: StringFromHex
                  - text: ", and"
                  - code [ref=e285]: StringFromUriComponent
                  - text: schemas for decoding encoded strings into UTF-8, improved
                  - code [ref=e286]: Schema.encodeKeys
                  - text: typing, fixed several missing
                  - code [ref=e287]: ~rebuild.out
                  - text: types, fixed duplicate identifier reference reuse, added a compile-time
                  - code [ref=e288]: MissingSelfGeneric
                  - text: error for Class APIs, and fixed
                  - code [ref=e289]: "@category"
                  - text: JSDoc tags.
              - listitem [ref=e290]:
                - paragraph [ref=e291]:
                  - strong [ref=e292]: RPC & Entity
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e293]: rpc
                  - code [ref=e294]: ConnectionHooks
                  - text: ", fixed"
                  - code [ref=e295]: Entity.keepAlive
                  - text: ", and improved defect handling when building Entity handlers."
              - listitem [ref=e296]:
                - paragraph [ref=e297]:
                  - strong [ref=e298]: Runtime & type inference
                  - text: ": Used"
                  - code [ref=e299]: NoInfer
                  - text: in
                  - code [ref=e300]: Layer
                  - text: constructors to prevent type erasure, fixed cache constructor inference by moving the
                  - code [ref=e301]: lookup
                  - text: option, fixed type inference for
                  - code [ref=e302]: Effect.retry
                  - text: when the
                  - code [ref=e303]: times
                  - text: option is provided, and fixed
                  - code [ref=e304]: Stream.toQueue
                  - text: types.
              - listitem [ref=e305]:
                - paragraph [ref=e306]:
                  - strong [ref=e307]: CLI & HTTP
                  - text: ": Fixed unstable CLI boolean flags, underlined active labels in CLI multi-select prompts, added support for"
                  - code [ref=e308]: form-urlencoded
                  - text: in the OpenAPI generator’s HTTP client format, and added a docs section on customizing schema error responses in
                  - code [ref=e309]: HttpApi
                  - text: .
              - listitem [ref=e310]:

                  - strong [ref=e312]: PostgreSQL & internals

                  - code [ref=e313]: PgClient.fromPool
                  - text: ", returned resolvers directly from"
                  - code [ref=e314]: SqlModel.makeResolvers
                  - text: ", added"
                  - code [ref=e315]: KeyValueStore.layerSql
                  - text: ", and made"
                  - code [ref=e316]: Unify.unify
                  - text: work with the
                  - code [ref=e317]: Layer
                  - text: module.

            - paragraph [ref=e234]: Hi Effecters!
            - paragraph [ref=e235]:
              - text: Welcome back to This Week In Effect (
              - strong [ref=e236]: TWIE
              - text: ) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.
            - paragraph [ref=e237]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.
            - paragraph [ref=e238]: To get started, below you’ll find links to our documentation as well as our guide for installing Effect. Enjoy!

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

            - paragraph [ref=e229]:

            - paragraph [ref=e231]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-04-17
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-04-17

**Date:** Apr 17, 2026
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio (Project Manager), Mirela Prifti (Community Manager), Michael Arnaldi (BDFL)
**Tags:** This Week In Effect
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/04/17/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with [`@effect/workflow`](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/blob/main/packages/workflow/README.md) - currently in alpha.
- 6270+ community members on [Discord](https://discord.gg/effect-ts).

---

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

Here are the main changes that landed in `effect-smol`:

- **Schema refinements**: Renamed `~rebuild.out` to `Rebuild` for cleaner ergonomics, added `Schema.annotateEncoded` for annotating the encoded side of a schema, added `withDecodingDefaultTypeKey`/`withDecodingDefaultType`, and allowed Class constructors to accept `void` when all fields are optional.
- **RPC improvements**: Added support for deferred responses in RPC, added `RpcGroup.omit`, and added a `require` option to `AtomRpc.query` to make atoms serializable.
- **IndexedDB persistence**: Merged the IDB persistence layer, building on the IndexedDB module introduced last week.
- **HttpApi & HTTP**: Added support for common string literals in `HttpApiSchema.status`, and omitted scope from `HttpApi` handlers to simplify usage.
- **EventLog**: `EventLog` identity now string-encodes to base64.
- **CLI**: Fixed an issue with exported CLI completion types.
- **Internals**: Fixed SIGINT listener not being removed until fiber exit, updated dependencies, and cleaned up `AGENTS.md`.

---

## Community Highlights

- Tim Smart released [effect-genserver](https://github.com/tim-smart/effect-genserver), bringing GenServer-style actor semantics to Effect. Compatible with Effect Cluster, RPC, and Effect Atom.
- Effect just crossed 10m weekly downloads! 🔥 (Michael Arnaldi's tweet)
- Matt Pocock: "I use Effect" - Production-grade taste.
- Micha Hernandez van Leuffen shared how [Fiberplane](https://fiberplane.com/) has been using Claude Code alongside Effect for typed errors and dependency management.
- Sebastien praised the [Effect Institute](https://www.effect.institute/) site.
- [svelte-effect-runtime](https://github.com/usebarekey/svelte-effect-runtime) – Effect Runtime integration for Svelte.
- [kbroom-nvim](https://github.com/ggallovalle/kbroom-nvim) – Neovim config showcasing Effect-inspired patterns.
- [effect-encore](https://github.com/cevr/effect-encore) – TypeScript library for building Encore.ts applications with Effect.
- [Effect HTTP API Course](https://www.effective.software/courses/effect-http-api) – newly published course.
- [effect-prodigi](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-prodigi) – Type-safe bindings for the Prodigi print-on-demand REST API.
- [Effect for TypeScript Developers](https://tonytangdev.github.io/effect-for-ts-developers/) – a 33-step learning guide.

## Effect Content Update

- Effect Office Hours 25 🔥
- Intro to Software Transactional Memory (STM)
- Roasting Tim Smart's OpenCode PR: refactor effect runtime

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at [Warp](https://www.joinwarp.com/), joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect to bring structure and composability to a growing TypeScript codebase, enabling a small, fast-moving team to stay productive while shipping reliable payment & payroll systems.

params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-04-10
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-04-10

**Date:** Apr 10, 2026
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio (Project Manager), Mirela Prifti (Community Manager), Michael Arnaldi (BDFL)
**Tags:** This Week In Effect
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/04/10/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with [`@effect/workflow`](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/blob/main/packages/workflow/README.md) - currently in alpha.
- 6250+ community members on [Discord](https://discord.gg/effect-ts).

---

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

Here are the main changes that landed in `effect-smol`:

- **Breaking: `ServiceMap` renamed to `Context`**: The `ServiceMap` module has been renamed back to `Context`, realigning with Effect v3 naming conventions. This is one of the most impactful changes of the week for anyone already building on the v4 beta.
- **IndexedDB expansion**: Added streaming and offset support to IDB queries, compound index improvements, encoded types for IDB queries, a `.reverse()` method to IDB selects, a `rebuild` API for IDB databases, an improved transaction API, and support for customizing IDB durability. `Model.Class` can now also be used directly for IndexedDB schemas.
- **Schema additions & fixes**: Added `StringFromBase64`, `StringFromBase64Url`, `StringFromHex`, and `StringFromUriComponent` schemas for decoding encoded strings into UTF-8, improved `Schema.encodeKeys` typing, fixed several missing `~rebuild.out` types, fixed duplicate identifier reference reuse, added a compile-time `MissingSelfGeneric` error for Class APIs, and fixed `@category` JSDoc tags.
- **RPC & Entity**: Added `rpc` `ConnectionHooks`, fixed `Entity.keepAlive`, and improved defect handling when building Entity handlers.
- **Runtime & type inference**: Used `NoInfer` in `Layer` constructors to prevent type erasure, fixed cache constructor inference by moving the `lookup` option, fixed type inference for `Effect.retry` when the `times` option is provided, and fixed `Stream.toQueue` types.
- **CLI & HTTP**: Fixed unstable CLI boolean flags, underlined active labels in CLI multi-select prompts, added support for `form-urlencoded` in the OpenAPI generator's HTTP client format, and added a docs section on customizing schema error responses in `HttpApi`.
- **PostgreSQL & internals**: Guarded transaction acquire failures in `PgClient.fromPool`, returned resolvers directly from `SqlModel.makeResolvers`, added `KeyValueStore.layerSql`, and made `Unify.unify` work with the `Layer` module.

---

## Community Highlights

- Michael Arnaldi ran an Effect workshop at the AI Engineer conference in London (Vibe Engineering with Effect).
- [effect-sql-model](https://github.com/emergente-labs/effect-sql-model) – an AST-based compiler that turns Effect Schema / @effect/sql model definitions into Drizzle ORM tables. Project by Emergente Labs.
- [spana](https://github.com/wezter96/spana) – a TypeScript-native end-to-end testing framework for cross-platform test automation across React Native and web. Project by wezter96.
- [@catenarycloud/linteffect](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@catenarycloud/linteffect) – Biome Grit rules for declarative Effect TypeScript composition. Project by Roman Naumenko.
- [effect-autoagent](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-autoagent) – a framework for building, optimizing, and deploying AI agents as Effect services. Project by Marc Suesser.
- [effect-claudecode](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-claudecode) – Write Claude Code plugins with Effect v4. Project by Marc Suesser.

## Effect Content Update

- Effect Office Hours 24 🔥
- TypeScript-Go with Effect LSP: Setup guide, features, and performance boost.

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at [Warp](https://www.joinwarp.com/), joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect to bring structure and composability to a growing TypeScript codebase, enabling a small, fast-moving team to stay productive while shipping reliable payment & payroll systems.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by navigating to the Effect blog to scrape the articles, and also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas for Person objects.

            - paragraph [ref=e228]: Hi Effecters!
            - paragraph [ref=e229]:
              - text: Welcome back to This Week In Effect (
              - strong [ref=e230]: TWIE
              - text: ) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.
            - paragraph [ref=e231]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.
            - paragraph [ref=e232]: To get started, below you’ll find links to our documentation as well as our guide for installing Effect. Enjoy!

params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-04-17
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-04-17

**Date:** Apr 17, 2026
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio (Project Manager), Mirela Prifti (Community Manager), Michael Arnaldi (BDFL)
**Tags:** This Week In Effect
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/04/17/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with [`@effect/workflow`](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/blob/main/packages/workflow/README.md) - currently in alpha.
- 6270+ community members on [Discord](https://discord.gg/effect-ts).

---

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

Here are the main changes that landed in `effect-smol`:

- **Schema refinements**: Renamed `~rebuild.out` to `Rebuild` for cleaner ergonomics, added `Schema.annotateEncoded` for annotating the encoded side of a schema, added `withDecodingDefaultTypeKey`/`withDecodingDefaultType`, and allowed Class constructors to accept `void` when all fields are optional.
- **RPC improvements**: Added support for deferred responses in RPC, added `RpcGroup.omit`, and added a `require` option to `AtomRpc.query` to make atoms serializable.
- **IndexedDB persistence**: Merged the IDB persistence layer, building on the IndexedDB module introduced last week.
- **HttpApi & HTTP**: Added support for common string literals in `HttpApiSchema.status`, and omitted scope from `HttpApi` handlers to simplify usage.
- **EventLog**: `EventLog` identity now string-encodes to base64.
- **CLI**: Fixed an issue with exported CLI completion types.
- **Internals**: Fixed SIGINT listener not being removed until fiber exit, updated dependencies, and cleaned up `AGENTS.md`.

---

## Community Highlights

- Tim Smart released [effect-genserver](https://github.com/tim-smart/effect-genserver), bringing GenServer-style actor semantics to Effect. Compatible with Effect Cluster, RPC, and Effect Atom.
- Effect just crossed 10m weekly downloads! 🔥 (Michael Arnaldi's tweet)
- Matt Pocock: "I use Effect" - Production-grade taste.
- Micha Hernandez van Leuffen shared how [Fiberplane](https://fiberplane.com/) has been using Claude Code alongside Effect for typed errors and dependency management.
- Sebastien praised the [Effect Institute](https://www.effect.institute/) site.
- [svelte-effect-runtime](https://github.com/usebarekey/svelte-effect-runtime) – Effect Runtime integration for Svelte.
- [kbroom-nvim](https://github.com/ggallovalle/kbroom-nvim) – Neovim config showcasing Effect-inspired patterns.
- [effect-encore](https://github.com/cevr/effect-encore) – TypeScript library for building Encore.ts applications with Effect.
- [Effect HTTP API Course](https://www.effective.software/courses/effect-http-api) – newly published course.
- [effect-prodigi](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-prodigi) – Type-safe bindings for the Prodigi print-on-demand REST API.
- [Effect for TypeScript Developers](https://tonytangdev.github.io/effect-for-ts-developers/) – a 33-step learning guide.

## Effect Content Update

- Effect Office Hours 25 🔥
- Intro to Software Transactional Memory (STM)
- Roasting Tim Smart's OpenCode PR: refactor effect runtime

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at [Warp](https://www.joinwarp.com/), joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect to bring structure and composability to a growing TypeScript codebase, enabling a small, fast-moving team to stay productive while shipping reliable payment & payroll systems.

params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-04-10
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-04-10

**Date:** Apr 10, 2026
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio (Project Manager), Mirela Prifti (Community Manager), Michael Arnaldi (BDFL)
**Tags:** This Week In Effect
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/04/10/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with [`@effect/workflow`](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/blob/main/packages/workflow/README.md) - currently in alpha.
- 6250+ community members on [Discord](https://discord.gg/effect-ts).

---

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

Here are the main changes that landed in `effect-smol`:

- **Breaking: `ServiceMap` renamed to `Context`**: The `ServiceMap` module has been renamed back to `Context`, realigning with Effect v3 naming conventions. This is one of the most impactful changes of the week for anyone already building on the v4 beta.
- **IndexedDB expansion**: Added streaming and offset support to IDB queries, compound index improvements, encoded types for IDB queries, a `.reverse()` method to IDB selects, a `rebuild` API for IDB databases, an improved transaction API, and support for customizing IDB durability. `Model.Class` can now also be used directly for IndexedDB schemas.
- **Schema additions & fixes**: Added `StringFromBase64`, `StringFromBase64Url`, `StringFromHex`, and `StringFromUriComponent` schemas for decoding encoded strings into UTF-8, improved `Schema.encodeKeys` typing, fixed several missing `~rebuild.out` types, fixed duplicate identifier reference reuse, added a compile-time `MissingSelfGeneric` error for Class APIs, and fixed `@category` JSDoc tags.
- **RPC & Entity**: Added `rpc` `ConnectionHooks`, fixed `Entity.keepAlive`, and improved defect handling when building Entity handlers.
- **Runtime & type inference**: Used `NoInfer` in `Layer` constructors to prevent type erasure, fixed cache constructor inference by moving the `lookup` option, fixed type inference for `Effect.retry` when the `times` option is provided, and fixed `Stream.toQueue` types.
- **CLI & HTTP**: Fixed unstable CLI boolean flags, underlined active labels in CLI multi-select prompts, added support for `form-urlencoded` in the OpenAPI generator's HTTP client format, and added a docs section on customizing schema error responses in `HttpApi`.
- **PostgreSQL & internals**: Guarded transaction acquire failures in `PgClient.fromPool`, returned resolvers directly from `SqlModel.makeResolvers`, added `KeyValueStore.layerSql`, and made `Unify.unify` work with the `Layer` module.

---

## Community Highlights

- Michael Arnaldi ran an Effect workshop at the AI Engineer conference in London (Vibe Engineering with Effect).
- [effect-sql-model](https://github.com/emergente-labs/effect-sql-model) – an AST-based compiler that turns Effect Schema / @effect/sql model definitions into Drizzle ORM tables. Project by Emergente Labs.
- [spana](https://github.com/wezter96/spana) – a TypeScript-native end-to-end testing framework for cross-platform test automation across React Native and web. Project by wezter96.
- [@catenarycloud/linteffect](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@catenarycloud/linteffect) – Biome Grit rules for declarative Effect TypeScript composition. Project by Roman Naumenko.
- [effect-autoagent](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-autoagent) – a framework for building, optimizing, and deploying AI agents as Effect services. Project by Marc Suesser.
- [effect-claudecode](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-claudecode) – Write Claude Code plugins with Effect v4. Project by Marc Suesser.

## Effect Content Update

- Effect Office Hours 24 🔥
- TypeScript-Go with Effect LSP: Setup guide, features, and performance boost.

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at [Warp](https://www.joinwarp.com/), joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect to bring structure and composability to a growing TypeScript codebase, enabling a small, fast-moving team to stay productive while shipping reliable payment & payroll systems.

params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-04-03
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-04-03

**Date:** Apr 3, 2026
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio (Project Manager), Mirela Prifti (Community Manager), Michael Arnaldi (BDFL)
**Tags:** This Week In Effect
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/04/03/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with [`@effect/workflow`](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/blob/main/packages/workflow/README.md) - currently in alpha.
- 6230+ community members on [Discord](https://discord.gg/effect-ts).

---

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

Here are the main changes that landed in `effect-smol`:

- **Schema overhaul**: Renamed `makeUnsafe` back to `make`, merged `ExtendableClass` into `Class`, added `Schema.asClass` to turn any schema into a class with static method support, added `Schema.resolveAnnotationsKey`, added a compile-time `MissingSelfGeneric` error for Class APIs, simplified default-value APIs to accept `Effect<T>`, added `makeEffect` alongside `makeUnsafe` on `Schema.Bottom`, removed the `~annotate.in` type from `Bottom`, fixed duplicate identifier reuse in references, fixed `ErrorClass`/`TaggedErrorClass` toString to match native Error output, and added several missing `*FromString` schemas.
- **IndexedDB & reactive stores**: Merged the long-awaited `IndexedDb` module (originally opened in February), added `.reactive` to IndexedDB `.first` queries, added defaults to reactivity keys, allowed `Model.Class` for IndexedDB schemas, and allowed customizing IDB durability.
- **Stream & runtime**: Added `Stream` service accessors, fixed `Stream.groupedWithin` partial batch flushing, added `Stream.timeoutOrElse`, fixed `Effect.repeat` to use the effect return value when using options, and added `Layer.suspend` constructor.
- **SQL & HTTP**: Added `KeyValueStore.layerSql`, returned resolvers directly from `SqlModel.makeResolvers`, made `Unify.unify` work with the `Layer` module, and updated the `HttpMiddleware` logger to only log the path.
- **Platform & internals**: Added child process `unref` support, cleaned up `ShardId`, removed rulesync and cleanup patterns, and renamed `dtslint` directories to `typetest`.

### Effect AI

- [Fix(ai-openrouter): correct HTTP-Referrer header to HTTP-Referer](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/pull/6145) (Bug Fix)

### Effect CLI

- [Fix(cli): prevent —log-level=value from swallowing next argument](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/pull/6144) (Bug Fix)

---

## Community Highlights

- Michael Arnaldi teased what Tim's been cooking with a cryptic commit log screenshot 👀
- Tim Smart: "Effect HttpClient is the correct choice" – in response to an axios supply chain attack alert.
- Dillon Mulroy building something cool around Cloudflare tunnels with Effect 4 beta.
- Fiberplane shared how they use Effect, ast-grep, and drift to build a self-driving codebase.
- Aiden Bai shared that [Expect](https://twitter.com/aidenybai/status/2037244695487930443) is mostly written in Effect.ts.
- Harry Solovay: "Reducer patterns compose more beautifully with Effect."
- Nele Lea: For self-driving codebases, more explicit code matters. Tools like Effect can help by making code easier for agents and humans.
- Dillon Mulroy beautifully highlights the role of Effect in the OpenCode codebase.
- The [Effect Foundation](https://www.effective.software/courses/effect-foundation) course got an update for Effect Services and Layers.
- [github-stars-organizer](https://github.com/davidnussio/github-stars-organizer) – a coffee-break project done with a single prompt, Opus 4.6 + Effect MCP. By David Nussio.
- [lion](https://github.com/andrueandersoncs/lion) – a JSON-based Lisp written 100% in Effect.
- [effect-analyzer](https://jagreehal.github.io/effect-analyzer/) – browser-based static analysis tool for Effect code. By Jag Reehal.
- [effect-skills](https://github.com/betalyra/effect-skills) – opinionated best-practice guidelines for Effect-TS codebases. By Betalyra.
- [sveltekit-effect-runtime](https://github.com/RATIU5/sveltekit-effect-runtime) – runtime-only adapter for using Effect within SvelteKit. By RATIU5.
- [effect-oxlint](https://github.com/mpsuesser/effect-oxlint) – library for writing oxlint custom lint rules using Effect v4 patterns. By mpsuesser.
- [opencode-effect-enforcer](https://github.com/mpsuesser/opencode-effect-enforcer) – OpenCode plugin that enforces Effect v4 development guardrails. By mpsuesser.
- [foldkit.dev/foldkit-vs-react-side-by-side](https://foldkit.dev/foldkit-vs-react-side-by-side) – side-by-side comparison of a pixel art editor in Foldkit and React. By Devin Jameson.

## Effect Content Update

- Effect Office Hours 23 🔥
- Effect.Service
- Mike's workflow for coding with agents
- Tips on convincing co-workers to use Effect

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at [Warp](https://www.joinwarp.com/), joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect to bring structure and composability to a growing TypeScript codebase, enabling a small, fast-moving team to stay productive while shipping reliable payment & payroll systems.
